mirror_policy: The root mirror must remain byte-for-byte identical to this registry until the root policy hierarchy is formally amended.

rules.title: Task Contract Required Before Execution
rules.description: Meaningful implementation work must not begin without a governing task contract that identifies the task, change class, approval class, authorized scope, required outputs, documentation impact, release impact, validation gates, and reviewer roles.
rules.validation: Confirm a task contract exists before implementation and that it contains the required governance fields.
rules.remediation: Create or amend the task contract before continuing implementation.

rules.title: Scope Boundary Enforcement
rules.description: Changed files must stay within the authorized scope defined by the task contract. If additional files are required, the contract must be amended before those files are modified.
rules.validation: Compare changed files against the governing contract scope.
rules.remediation: Remove unauthorized changes or amend the contract before proceeding.

rules.title: Role Separation Enforcement
rules.description: A role must not perform actions assigned to another role. Builders must not validate their own work. Validators must not implement fixes. Release Managers must not waive compliance gates. Role authority boundaries must match the repository role model.
rules.validation: Confirm each action was performed by a role authorized for that action.
rules.remediation: Reassign the action to the correct role and obtain the required independent review.

rules.title: Protected Asset Governance Gate
rules.description: Protected governance assets must not be modified without the required approval class and Governance Steward authorization when governance-class authority is required.
rules.validation: Compare changed files against protected path policy and verify required approval evidence.
rules.remediation: Obtain the required authorization, amend the contract, or remove protected-path changes.

rules.title: Changelog Entry Required for Substantive Changes
rules.description: Every meaningful change must include a changelog entry with the required fields, accurate change class, accurate version impact, affected area, task reference, approval class, and migration guidance when the change is breaking.
rules.validation: Verify the changelog entry exists and matches the task contract and actual changes.
rules.remediation: Add or correct the changelog entry before review completes.

rules.title: Breaking Change Disclosure Mandate
rules.description: A change that alters existing governance rules, required fields, default behavior, schemas, workflow enforcement, or consumer obligations must be explicitly classified as breaking-change with major version impact unless the Release Manager documents a higher-authority exception.
required_evidence: major version impact or documented exception
rules.validation: Review the diff and contract for consumer-impacting changes and confirm breaking-change handling.
rules.remediation: Reclassify the change, add migration guidance, and obtain the required elevated review.

rules.title: Completion Summary Truthfulness
rules.description: Completion summaries must accurately describe files changed, validation results, known issues, documentation status, release impact, and scope compliance. Claims of done, tested, reviewed, or no issues must be supported by evidence.
rules.validation: Compare completion claims against the diff, validation output, and review evidence.
rules.remediation: Correct the completion summary and disclose partial completion, skipped checks, or unresolved issues.

rules.title: Documentation Sync Compliance
rules.description: Changes that affect documented behavior, policy, process, public interfaces, release history, or canonical documentation must update the corresponding documentation surfaces or explicitly track the sync obligation under the documentation policy.
required_evidence: sync marker or follow-up tracking when permitted
rules.validation: Check modified canonical sources against documentation sync rules and derived wiki pages.
rules.remediation: Update required documentation surfaces or create the required traceable follow-up before approval.

rules.title: Version Classification Accuracy
rules.description: Version impact must match the actual effect of the change. Breaking changes require major impact unless a documented higher-authority exception applies. Governance-only impact must not be used to hide consumer-facing breaking changes.
rules.validation: Compare the version impact against the changed files, policy meaning, and release policy.
rules.remediation: Correct the version impact and changelog entry, then obtain release review when required.

rules.title: Governance Change Isolation
rules.description: Governance changes must be standalone. A governance or protected policy change must not be bundled with unrelated feature, bugfix, refactor, release, adapter, or implementation work.
rules.validation: Inspect the changed files and task contract for unrelated non-governance work.
rules.remediation: Split unrelated changes into separate task contracts and branches.

rules.title: Evidence and Validation Integrity
rules.description: Validation evidence must be real, specific, current, and traceable to commands, tools, reports, or reviewer decisions that actually ran. Failed, skipped, partial, stale, or unavailable validation must be disclosed.
rules.validation: Verify evidence files and reported validation outcomes against actual command output and reviewer records.
rules.remediation: Run the missing validation, correct stale evidence, or disclose the limitation and remediation plan.

rules.title: Accountability and Non-Attribution
rules.description: Assisted work must never replace accountable human ownership or governed role accountability. Attribution credit to tools, models, vendors, automation, or agents is prohibited in source files, generated code comments, commit messages, changelog entries, release notes, README notices, contributor lists, authorship metadata, and documentation prose.
required_evidence: task contract or remediation phase reference
rules.validation: Confirm accountable ownership, contract or phase traceability, review evidence, validation evidence, required approval evidence, and absence of prohibited attribution credit.
rules.remediation: Remove prohibited attribution credit and add missing accountability, review, validation, or required approval evidence.
